title: Ash Peak Dungeon
content:
Approach - 2 Goblins stand guard. They're mad they didn't get to participate in the main fight, but are on guard.

Guard Post - The bugbear who stole the young girl was injured while fleeing, and is being treated by a captured shaman named Burta. The shaman is under duress and heals them against her will. The shaman may notice the party before the bugbear does, and will assist them if the make an attempt at the Bugbears life. The shaman knows the following information:

- A small party of goblins took her from Daermill three weeks ago. 
- The goblins here seem to have come from  all around, travelling in small packs and gathering in the mountain over time.
- The goblins are led by a red dragonborn, though he has not interacted much with the shaman
- The girl was brought in recently and rushed off toward the lair

Nearby, the gates to the lair stand shut. A complex locking mechanism blocks movement forward. Examining the mechanism closely, a DC 17 History check shows the Cant emblem of Cormin Kingslayer, contracted to design an impenetrable lock. Only a DC 30 Lockpicking check unlocks the door without the key that goes with it.  

Storage room - A large ammount of stockpiled food and other supplies. A hidden door is located behind a cache of barrels and crates, leading to the resurrection laboratory.
An investigation check reveals trinkets from the following table: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1v5sVgsxUIwGcZ0czsmH2wHYxFlADv5SIzNjWAJsfNJ4/edit?gid=0#gid=0
10-14 - 1 Trinket
15-20 - 2 Trinkets and the Secret Entrance is Revealed
21+ - 3 Trinkets and the secret entrance is revealed

Resurrection Laboratory - Endrostae has done much research into resurrection methods, and while he is not capable of bringing back Igenvar, he is a cunning dragonborn eager to find workarounds. Hanging upside down in this secret laboratory is a horse with brilliant white hair. The beast is drained of all its blood, seemingly through a large circular wound on its forehead. A DC 15 Nature or Religion check reveals that the beast is in fact a celestial unicorn. A *detect evil and good* or divine sense reveals the same. The blood and horns of unicorns have potent magical properties, allowing mages to accomplish feats far beyond their natural abilities.
A set of holy texts and arcane grimoires are arrayed on a workbench nearby. The cramped space is also filled with alchemy tools and reagents. A number of unfinished scrolls litter the place, though a complete *scroll of revivify* lays nearby A DC 10 Arcana check reveals that research into spells is occuring here. On a 15 or higher, the scroll is identified as actually a failed attempt to create a much more powerful resurrection scroll. 

Barracks - The handful of goblins, 2 scouts and a bugbear, rest here. They can either be snuck by or eliminated while going to the Endrostae's room. The corpses of two dead guards sit in the corner, remanants of the small force that the king stationed here.

Endrostae's Quarters - Endrostae is out leading the invasion force, but he began returning as soon as the girl was kidnapped. His quarters are immaculately well-maintained, due in part to the goblins' crippling fear of him. A large bed, dresser, and desk occupy most of the space in the room. A small, locked footlocker sits at the foot of the bed. Inside are 3 gold bars worth 50 GP each, a magical whetstone, and a scroll of contact other plane. The scroll only contacts Tiamat.
The whetstone enhances a non-magical slashing or piercing weapon to a +1 weapon for 1 hour when it is sharpened with it for a minute. This effect can only be used once per day.
A secret compartment in the desk hides Endrostae's notes. A diary of sorts written entirely in High Draconic, it dictates his attempts at creating the resurrection spells in the laboratory. In between more serious technical work, it is clear the author has a searing hatred of humanity. 
In the dresser is a set of well maintained half-plate armor, with decals of chromatic dragons inlaid.
A DC 12 Perception check reveals a hidden panel under the bed that even Endrostae did not notice.

Secret Lair - A pair of Darklurkers make this large cavern home, feeding on the vermin that crawl in and out of the lair. 

Secret cavern

subscribergroups.title: Adventurers of Test World Please Ignore Party
